MTN FOR STAY OF PROCEEDINGS FILED BY DEFENDANT HEMMING, PAUL AN INDIVIDUAL TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB, A BUSINESS ENTITY, FORM ENTITY FORM UNKNOWN HEARING SET FOR JAN-30-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS PAUL HEMMING AND TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB'S MOTION FOR STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. FIRST, DEFENDANT'S MOTION HAS NO EVIDENCE AND DEFENDANT FORGOT TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE REHABILITATION AND INJUNCTION ORDER ISSUED ON NOVEMBER 7, 2013. SECOND, CCP 1064.9 DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS CASE BECAUSE IT IS NOT AN "ACTION OR PROCEEDING IN THE NATURE OF AN ATTACHMENT, GARNISHMENT, OR EXECUTION." IN HAWTHORNE SAVINGS F.S.B. V. RELIANCE INS. CO. OF ILLINOIS (2005) 421 F.3D 835, 849 (AMENDED AND SUPERSEDED BY HAWTHORNE SAVINGS F.S.B. V. RELIANCE INS. CO. OF ILLINOIS (9TH CIR. 2006) 433 F.3D 1089, THE COURT EXPLAINS THAT A PURPOSE OF A STAY IS TO PROHIBIT ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS, NOT ADJUDICATION OF THE UNDERLYING MERITS. THIRD, THE PRINCIPLE OF FULL FAITH AND CREDIT DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE DELAWARE PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT RECEIVED AND CONSIDERED DEFENDANTS' LATE REPLY BRIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

Mtn For Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANDREW MCDEVITT, ESQ., 415.981.7210, FOR PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN KENDALL.
JUSTIN WATKINS, ESQ., 702.562.6000, FOR DEFENDANTS TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB AND PAUL HEMMING.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS PAUL HEMMING AND TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB'S MOTION For Stay Of Proceedings is denied. First, defendant's motion has no evidence and defendant forgot to provide a copy of the rehabilitation and injunction order issued on November 7, 2013. Second, CCP 1064.9 does not apply to this case because it is not an "action or proceeding in the nature of an attachment, garnishment, or execution." In Hawthorne Savings F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Illinois (2005) 421 F.3d 835, 849 (amended and superseded by Hawthorne Savings F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Illinois (9th Cir. 2006) 433 F.3d 1089, the court explains that a purpose of a stay is to prohibit enforcement actions, not adjudication of the underlying merits. Third, the principle of full faith and credit does not apply because plaintiff was not a party to the Delaware proceedings. The Court received and considered defendants' late reply brief. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
